_comment: Full identity / IDOR / access-control spec TEMPLATE. Copy to a gitignored *.local.json, fill in REAL sessions/credentials for a host you are AUTHORIZED to test, and never commit secrets. One spec serves three scanners: AccessScannerMain runs the `cases` array; IdorScannerMain and SpaIdorScannerMain ignore `cases` and use the identities -- one named `attacker` (or the lone identity if there is exactly one) plus an optional `victim` -- to log in as both, harvest the victim's object ids, then try to read them while authenticated as the attacker. Each identity authenticates ONE of three ways: `login`, `cookie`, or `headers`. Placeholder hosts use the reserved `.example` TLD; replace everything in CAPS.

attacker._comment: Auth option A -- login: the scanner drives this one real login form (deterministic field detection; the only auto-submit it performs). This is the identity the IDOR scanners act AS.

victim._comment: Auth option B -- cookie: a raw Cookie header value copied from your browser devtools after logging in as the victim. Most reliable (no MFA / field-detection issues) and keeps passwords out of the file. For the two-identity IDOR test the victim only needs to be reachable so the scanner can harvest the victim's object ids to try as the attacker.

reader._comment: Auth option C -- headers: token auth, e.g. a bearer JWT. Same effect as `cookie`. Only used if a case below references it; delete this identity if you do not need it.

cases._comment: IDOR / broken object-level auth: request a VICTIM-owned object AS the attacker. A finding when the response is 2xx and contains data that should be restricted. Put a victim-owned id in `url` and a victim-specific string in `mustContain`.
cases.name: attacker reads the victim's order

cases._comment: Same check via the token-auth identity, to show `headers` in use.
cases.name: reader reads the victim's order via API token

cases._comment: Missing function-level auth: a privileged page requested with NO session. `identity: null` = unauthenticated. A finding if it returns 2xx containing `mustContain`.
cases.name: admin panel reachable without a session
